_meta.description: MMP Consent Extension v0.1.0 — Interop Test Vectors
_meta.author: Denis Yermakou <axonosorg@gmail.com>
notes: Each test case contains a canonical JSON frame (what the relay sees)
and the expected ConsentEngine state transitions.
Frame types are string identifiers per MMP Section 7.
Hongwei: feed the 'json' field to your Node.js frame parser.
If it doesn't throw, the interop surface is correct.

vectors.name: consent-withdraw: peer scope, user initiated
vectors.description: Basic peer withdrawal. The most common case — user presses disconnect.

vectors.name: consent-withdraw: all peers, safety violation, real-time timestamps
vectors.description: Emergency withdrawal from all peers. Uses timestamp_us (takes precedence over timestamp). Epoch field present for swarm-synchronised implementations.

enforcement.note: scope:all — withdrawing node sends to ALL connected peers. Delivery is asynchronous — local enforcement completes first.

vectors.name: consent-withdraw: AxonOS StimGuard lockout (implementation-specific reason 0x10)
vectors.description: Withdrawal triggered by Cognitive Hypervisor, not user. Repeated charge density violations caused StimGuard to initiate consent withdrawal autonomously.

json.reason: stimguard lockout: repeated charge density violations

vectors.name: consent-suspend: focus mode
vectors.description: User pauses coupling temporarily. Connection maintained, cognitive frames filtered.

enforcement.note: Peer MUST NOT send cognitive frames (state-sync, memory-share, mood, xmesh-insight). Transport frames (ping/pong, message, peer-info) continue.

vectors.name: consent-suspend: minimal fields
vectors.description: Suspend with no optional fields. Valid per spec — reasonCode and reason are OPTIONAL.

vectors.name: consent-resume: after suspension
vectors.description: Resumes coupling. Peer SHOULD send state-sync immediately after receiving this. Cold re-init of coupling engine expected (Section 3.3).

enforcement.note: First state-sync after resume triggers cold re-init of coupling engine. Higher latency expected until steady-state.

vectors.name: consent-withdraw: from suspended state
vectors.description: Withdrawal from SUSPENDED is valid — goes directly to WITHDRAWN (terminal). Section 4 state machine.

vectors.name: edge case: duplicate consent-suspend (idempotent)
vectors.description: A second consent-suspend while already suspended is a no-op. MUST NOT treat as error. Section 4.2.

vectors.name: edge case: consent-resume while already granted (idempotent)
vectors.description: A consent-resume from GRANTED state (never suspended) is silently ignored. Section 4.2.

vectors.name: edge case: consent-withdraw with hardware fault reason
vectors.description: Hardware-level fault detected. reasonCode 0x03. No human-readable reason — constrained implementation may omit string.

vectors.name: edge case: consent-withdraw with unknown reason code
vectors.description: reasonCode 0xFF — not in spec registry or AxonOS registry. Implementation MUST accept gracefully (no crash). Treated as unspecified.
vectors.expected_behavior: accept frame, treat reasonCode as unspecified, proceed with withdrawal

vectors.name: edge case: timestamp_us takes precedence over timestamp
vectors.description: Both timestamp (ms) and timestamp_us (µs) present. Per spec, timestamp_us takes precedence. Implementations using ms resolution MAY ignore timestamp_us.
vectors.expected_behavior: use timestamp_us (1711540800123456) as canonical time. timestamp (1711540800000) is informational backup.

vectors.name: gossip encoding: 2-bit consent state for BLE ATT MTU constraint
vectors.description: Compact consent state encoding for peer-info gossip frames. Section 6.4.

vectors.name: AxonOS-specific: emergency button bypass (reasonCode 0x12)
vectors.description: Physical emergency button pressed. Bypasses NSC gateway — direct interrupt to Secure World. ConsentEngine + StimGuard withdrawal in <1µs.

enforcement.enforcement_path: direct interrupt → Secure World → ConsentEngine.set_withdrawn() → StimGuard.consent_withdrawn() → GPIO DAC gate close

conformance_checklist._note: Section 10 conformance items. Each test vector maps to one or more items.
general: 1. Implement three frame types (consent-withdraw, consent-suspend, consent-resume)
3. Enforce local withdrawal before notification (steps 1-4 before 5-6)
4. Check consent state before any coupling evaluation
5. Filter cognitive frames during SUSPENDED state
6. Handle duplicate consent frames as no-ops (TV-009, TV-010)
7. Treat connection loss during SUSPENDED as WITHDRAWN
8. Silently ignore consent frames if extension not supported
safety_critical: 10. Persist consent state to NVRAM before notification
11. Prevent auto-reconnect to WITHDRAWN peers after power-cycle
12. Document failure mode when persistence unavailable
